title: Crispy Caramelized German Chicken Skewers
description: A lazy Sunday German dish featuring chicken thighs, jammy and snappy, perfect for a low-carb friendly meal. This recipe uses a spherification technique to add a unique twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken thighs to the bowl and toss to coat evenly.
Thread the marinated chicken thighs onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, mix the sodium alginate with water in a small bowl.
After the chicken has baked for 25 minutes, brush the sodium alginate mixture evenly onto each skewer.
Return the skewers to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until caramelized and crispy.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
